hallucinations.description: All reported experimental results (94.4% variance reduction, 93.8% F1, 95% propagation rate, etc.) are generated by hardcoded simulation functions in baselines.py and evaluation.py rather than from any real experiments or data. The simulation code explicitly sets target metrics to match paper claims.
hallucinations.evidence: In baselines.py: ReproducibilityEvaluator.simulate_reproduction_experiment hardcodes success_rate=0.92/0.68 and variance=0.02/0.08; ImpactTracingEvaluator and AnnotationPropagationEvaluator similarly hardcode all reported numbers.

hallucinations.description: Multiple references cite nonexistent or future-dated arXiv papers (e.g., SAVeD arXiv:2511.17298 from 2025, CC30k arXiv:2511.07790 from 2025, and others with 2025 dates).
hallucinations.evidence: References [4], [9], [10] in the paper use arXiv IDs and years that do not correspond to real publications.

overall_assessment: Hallucinations found: fabricated experimental results via simulation and multiple nonexistent/future citations. The paper presents simulated outputs as empirical validation.
